element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary melee combat tool throughout game
Limited supply resource, used for crowd control and ranged threats
Progressive wave density and enemy variety increases across six levels
Enemy type complexity and tactical variety escalates through progression

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade arcade-style wave-based pacing with escalating difficulty across six distinct level stages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory wave combat teaching basic kunai and shuriken mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy wave density and variety across middle levels
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-wave encounters in final levels with maximum enemy presence
notable_spikes: Transition from tutorial waves to aggressive enemy patterns
Resource management pressure from limited shuriken supply in later stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level presents multiple waves of Banglar forces with increasing difficulty. Players fight through successive waves with brief respites between encounters.
Two playable characters (Kunoichi and Ninja) with identical movesets but distinct visual/thematic presentation as android warriors
Long-range shuriken attacks limited in supply, forcing players to balance range and melee combat throughout encounters
